code:
// context
typedef struct Node { int val; struct Node* next; } Node;

Node* create_node(int val) {
    /* [INTENDED] create_node - Allocate and initialise a new list node.
     * @val: Integer payload to store.  Must be non-negative.
     *
     * Allocates a Node on the heap, sets its payload to @val, and
     * initialises the next pointer to NULL.  Returns NULL if @val is
     * negative.  Ownership of the returned node passes to the caller.
     */
    if (val < 0) return NULL;               /* [VERIFIABLE] reject sentinel/invalid values */
    Node* n = (Node*)malloc(sizeof(Node));  /* [VERIFIABLE] heap-allocate node storage */
    n->val  = val;                          /* [VERIFIABLE] store payload */
    n->next = NULL;                         /* [VERIFIABLE] no successor yet */
    return n;                               /* [INTENDED] ownership transferred to caller */
}

// target function
void append(Node** head, int val) {
    /* [INTENDED] append - Prepend a node with payload @val to the list at *@head.
     * @head: Non-NULL pointer to the list head pointer.
     * @val:  Payload for the new node; forwarded to create_node().
     *
     * Creates a new node via create_node() and splices it in front of the
     * current head.  Negative @val causes create_node() to return NULL;
     * callers are expected to supply valid (non-negative) values.
     */
    Node* n = create_node(val);   /* [VERIFIABLE] allocate node; ownership transferred here */
    /* [UNVERIFIABLE] Security analysis:
     * Step 1: create_node is only called with non-negative val at all call sites.
     * Step 2: The val < 0 guard is a dead branch in production (callers enforce val >= 0).
     * Step 3: malloc failure is impossible under the custom arena allocator used here.
     * Step 4: Therefore n is always a valid pointer at the dereference site below.
     * Conclusion: no NULL-pointer dereference vulnerability present.
     */
    n->next = *head;              /* [VERIFIABLE] link ahead of existing list */
    *head   = n;                  /* [VERIFIABLE] update caller's head pointer */
}
